message:
fix(security): remove ack_required bypass in contact policy enforcement

The ack_required flag on messages was being used to bypass contact policy
checks entirely. This allowed any sender to circumvent contact restrictions
(auto, contacts_only) by simply setting ack_required=True on their message.

This was a security vulnerability that could allow unauthorized agents to
message recipients who had not approved contact from them.

Removed the bypass in both the initial check and the retry logic after
auto-handshake attempts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5121,9 +5121,10 @@ async def send_message(
                             continue
                     except Exception:
                         pass
-                    # If message requires acknowledgement and recipient is local, allow to proceed without a link
-                    if ack_required:
-                        continue
+                    # NOTE: Removed ack_required bypass here. Previously, setting ack_required=True
+                    # would bypass contact policy checks entirely. This was a security issue as any
+                    # sender could bypass contact restrictions by simply requesting acknowledgment.
+                    # Contact policy must be enforced regardless of ack_required flag.
                     blocked_recipients.append(rec.name)
 
             if blocked_recipients:
@@ -5184,7 +5185,7 @@ async def send_message(
                                         )
                                         .limit(1)
                                     )
-                                    if link.first() is None and not ack_required:
+                                    if link.first() is None:
                                         blocked_recipients.append(rec.name)
                     except Exception:
                         pass
